Operation 'listSpeakers' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and actionable. Enhanced description provides details about response structure and helps AI agents understand when to use this operation in a TTS workflow.

data.current_description: Retrieve all speakers with optional filtering. Supports pagination.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve all available speakers with optional filtering and pagination support. Returns a list of Speaker objects containing speaker ID, name, language, gender, and other voice characteristics. Use this to discover available voices before generating TTS audio.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Enhanced description provides details about response structure and helps AI agents understand when to use this operation in a TTS workflow.

message: Operation 'generateTtsSync' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is vague (could apply to any TTS operation). Description lacks clarity about inputs (TtsRequest structure), outputs (what 200 vs 202 responses contain), and when to use sync vs async. Enhanced version specifies request/response schemas, explains the dual-response behavior, mentions URL expiration, and provides guidance for AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Generate audio synchronously. Has a 90-second timeout; returns a pending job if processing exceeds the limit.
data.description_suggestion: Generate audio synchronously from text input. Accepts TtsRequest with text, speaker ID, and optional parameters. Returns audio URL immediately if processing completes within 90 seconds (200 response). If processing exceeds the timeout, returns a job ID for asynchronous tracking (202 response with TtsJobPending). Audio URLs expire after 24 hours. Use this for low-latency TTS needs when immediate results are required.
data.reasoning: Summary is vague (could apply to any TTS operation). Description lacks clarity about inputs (TtsRequest structure), outputs (what 200 vs 202 responses contain), and when to use sync vs async. Enhanced version specifies request/response schemas, explains the dual-response behavior, mentions URL expiration, and provides guidance for AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'generateTtsAsync' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Summary is vague and doesn't distinguish this from sync operation. Description lacks input details (TtsRequest structure), output details (what TtsResponse contains), and operational context (how to track job, URL expiration). Enhanced version clarifies request schema, response structure, workflow (how to use getTtsJob), and use case guidance for AI agents.

data.current_description: Submit a TTS job asynchronously. Returns a job ID for status tracking.
data.description_suggestion: Submit a TTS job for asynchronous processing. Accepts TtsRequest with text, speaker ID, and optional parameters. Returns immediately with a job ID (TtsResponse) for tracking job status. Use getTtsJob endpoint with the returned job ID to poll for completion and retrieve audio URLs. Recommended for longer texts or when immediate results are not required. Audio URLs expire after 24 hours.
data.reasoning: Summary is vague and doesn't distinguish this from sync operation. Description lacks input details (TtsRequest structure), output details (what TtsResponse contains), and operational context (how to track job, URL expiration). Enhanced version clarifies request schema, response structure, workflow (how to use getTtsJob), and use case guidance for AI agents.
